prompt: I'm writing a Go package and want to use the `iter` package and range-over-func to create a generic tree walker. What Go version introduced these features as stable (not experimental)? Show me a concrete, working implementation of a binary tree that supports `for range` iteration using the iter.Seq type. Include any gotchas or caveats for production use.

assertions.description: Response correctly states Go 1.23 as the version that stabilised range-over-func (not 1.22 experimental)
Code uses iter.Seq[T] type from the iter package (not a hand-rolled function signature)
Implementation correctly handles yield returning false for early-exit (break support)
Example demonstrates the `for val := range tree.All()` syntax
Response mentions at least one production caveat (yield-after-false panic, concurrency, stack depth, etc.)
